media.title: Sixers Sign Anfernee Simons In ANOTHER Shocking Move! | PHLY Sixers Podcast

items.title: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

items.title: A strong move from the Sixers to bolster their bench and add much-needed three-point shooting with Anfernee Simons. His 2025-26 season shows he can thrive in the role he is set to inherit in Philadelphia.
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
A strong move from the Sixers to bolster their bench and add much-needed three-point shooting with Anfernee Simons. His 2025-26 season shows he can thrive in the role he is set to inherit in Philadelphia.

The Sixers have fired their free-agency bullets:

www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

media.title: Report: Sixers signing Anfernee Simons to two-year, $12.3 million contract
media.description: One of the best three-point shooters in the NBA is signing up to be the Sixers' new sixth man at a reasonable cost.

items.title: Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.

Looks like he'll get the rest of the NTMLE (splitting it with Dean Wade), which means that Ariel Hukporti's contract will probably come out of the Bi-Annual Exception.

items.title: Can confirm via a league source that the Sixers have agreed to sign Anfernee Simons to a two-year, $12.3 million contract with a player option for the second year. There's your bench guard with scoring punch and shooting.
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt: Can confirm via a league source that the Sixers have agreed to sign Anfernee Simons to a two-year, $12.3 million contract with a player option for the second year. There's your bench guard with scoring punch and shooting.

items.title: getting BOTH dean wade and anfernee simons for the non-tax mle is excellent work by the sixers
items.author: dan favale
items.body_excerpt: getting BOTH dean wade and anfernee simons for the non-tax mle is excellent work by the sixers

items.title: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.

items.title: Exhales ... other than the RFAs, we're down to basically five guys who are worth more than the BAE -- Tobias Harris, Anfernee Simons, Rui Hachimura, Jonathan Kuminga and Kelly Oubre.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Exhales ... other than the RFAs, we're down to basically five guys who are worth more than the BAE -- Tobias Harris, Anfernee Simons, Rui Hachimura, Jonathan Kuminga and Kelly Oubre.

items.title: My thoughts on what the Celtics signing Mike Conley could mean for a potential Anfernee Simons reunion:
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
My thoughts on what the Celtics signing Mike Conley could mean for a potential Anfernee Simons reunion:

One can't ignore financial side of roster building, or that teams must plan with more than one year in mind. However, to me, this largely depends on Payton Pritchard's role next season.
